'''Danny Peacock''' (4 March 1968 – 24 July 2025) was an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He played for the [[Western Suburbs Magpies]], [[South Queensland Crushers]], [[Gold Coast Chargers#Gold Coast Seagulls|Gold Coast Seagulls]] and [[Bradford Bulls]] as a {{rlp|fb|ce}}, or {{rlp|wg}}.

==Playing career==
===Western Suburbs===
In the trials of the 1988 pre-season, it was said, "Another outstanding performer was winger Danny Peacock who continues to impress with some flashy footwork and heavy tackles."<ref>{{cite news| work= The Canberra Times | title=Top Sides Tumble in Trials|date=21 February 1988|page=21}}</ref> He made his debut for the [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Wests Magpies]] in the first round of the season, playing in the centres,<ref name="wests">{{cite web|title=Danny Peacock|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTktLTQtLS0tLS00NDItLS0tLS0tLS0=|accessdate=31 January 2010|publisher=Rugby League Project}}</ref> but was to play on the wing for most of the rest of the year. He scored his first points 3 weeks later, crossing for two tries in the round 4 match against [[Balmain Tigers|Balmain]].<ref name="wests" />

By 1989, Peacock was a regular in first grade, and was considered a, "great developing talent," noted for his, "sheer speed."<ref>{{cite news|author=Tony Sarno|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|title=Magpies batter Bears|url=https://news.google.com/newspapers?nid=1301&dat=19890731&id=8LRYAAAAIBAJ&sjid=nucDAAAAIBAJ&pg=4179,9658567|accessdate=1 January 2014|date=31 July 1989}}</ref>

Western Suburbs struggled in the late eighties, and Peacock played in just 10 winning games in the three years with club,<ref name="wests" /> but gained attention with his attacking play from fullback.

===Gold Coast and South Queensland===
Peacock joined the [[Gold Coast Chargers#Gold Coast Seagulls|Gold Coast Seagulls]], as they were then known, in 1991, the same year [[Wally Lewis]] joined the club. He quickly cemented the position as starting fullback. He spent 5 years with the club, and holds the record for the most tries scored for the club, with 28.<ref>{{cite web|title=NRL Heritage Round|url=http://www.bestbets.com.au/web/sport.afl.news.viewarticle.cmd?NEWS_ITEM_ID=12102|accessdate=31 January 2010|publisher=Best Bets}}{{Dead link|date=August 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

In 1995, Peacock was moved to the centres. He scored 14 tries that season, which is another club record.<ref name="rl 2009">Middleton, David (Editor); ''2009 Official Rugby League Annual''; published 2010 for the [[National Rugby League]] by News Magazines, Alexandria NSW, 2015</ref> He is also tenth on the list of most appearances for the club.<ref name="rl 2009" />

Peacock joined the [[South Queensland Crushers]] for the 1996 season, but only managed 11 appearances.

Peacock finished his first grade career with the unwanted record of 5 wooden spoons which he earned at the 3 different clubs he played for.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/seven-clubs-in-mix-for-nrl-wooden-spoon-and-the-most-unwanted-tag-in-rugby-league/news-story/082c6d690be0411e0d4059bc089e2f6c/|title=Seven clubs in mix for NRL wooden spoon and the most unwanted tag in rugby league|website=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney}}</ref>

===Bradford===
Moving to England in 1997, Peacock spent three seasons with the Bradford Bulls. Joining [[Graeme Bradley]] in the centres, he was a popular player at the club.<ref>{{cite web|title=The Millennium Masters – Backs|url=http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/content/History/3565/The%20Millennium%20Masters%20-%20Backs|accessdate=31 January 2010|publisher=Bradford Bulls|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20101004182902/http://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/content/History/3565/The%20Millennium%20Masters%20-%20Backs|archivedate=4 October 2010}}</ref> Coach Matt Elliott described him as, "an emotional leader for the team."<ref>{{cite news |title=Danny Boy gives lift |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/8062842.danny-boy-gives-lift/ |work=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |date=8 October 1999 |language=en}}</ref> Peacock was described as a "key figure" in the Bulls winning their 1997 title. He scored a try in the 1997 Challenge Cup Final as well as having one disallowed later in the first half.<ref name="farewell">{{cite news |title=Peacock gets big farewell |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/8064839.peacock-gets-big-farewell/ |access-date=20 May 2026 |work=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |date=20 May 1999 |language=en}}</ref>

Peacock retired in 1999 after a recurrence of a knee injury and was given a fitting farewell by the Bulls.<ref name="farewell" />

==Death==
Peacock died on 24 July 2025, at the age of 57.<ref>[https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/25340240.former-bradford-bulls-player-danny-peacock-dies-aged-57/ Former Bradford Bulls player Danny Peacock dies aged 57]</ref>
